steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recall the standard circle equation
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ satisfies:
$$(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$$
The right-hand side is the radius squared.

steps.title: Substitute the given center and radius
Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ${(x-14)}^{2}+{(y-19)}^{2}=36k: The right side is $36k$, not $(6k)^2 = 36k^2$. The radius was not properly squared. ✗
  • Choice B — ${(x-14)}^{2}+{(y-19)}^{2}=36{k}^{2}$: $(6k)^2 = 36k^2$. Center and radius are applied correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — ${(x-14)}^{2}+{(y-19)}^{2}=6k: The right side is just $6k$, which would mean $r = \sqrt{6k}$, not $6k$. ✗
  • Choice D — ${(x-14)}^{2}+{(y-19)}^{2}=6{k}^{2}$: $(6k)^2 = 36k^2$, not $6k^2$. Only $k$ was squared, not the full radius. ✗
That matches Choice B — ${(x-14)}^{2}+{(y-19)}^{2}=36{k}^{2}$.
